81→/**\n    82→ * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n    83→ * Trigger}.\n    84→ *\n    85→ * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n    86→ * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n    87→ * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n    88→ * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n    89→ * WindowAssigner}.\n    90→ *\n    91→ * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n    92→ * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n    93→ * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n    94→ * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n    95→ *\n    96→ * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n    97→ * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n    98→ * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n    99→ * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n   100→ */\n   101→@Internal\n   102→public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n   103→        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n   104→        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n   105→\n   106→    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n   107→\n   108→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   109→    // Configuration values and user functions\n   110→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   111→\n   112→    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n   113→\n   114→    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n   115→\n   116→    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n   117→\n   118→    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n   119→\n   120→    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n   121→    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n   122→\n   123→    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n   124→    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n   125→\n   126→    /**\n   127→     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n   128→     *\n   129→     * <ul>\n   130→     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n   131→     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n   132→     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n   133→     * </ul>\n   134→     */\n   135→    protected final long allowedLateness;\n   136→\n   137→    /**\n   138→     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n   139→     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n   140→     * to this.\n   141→     */\n   142→    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n   143→\n   144→    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n   145→\n   146→    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n   147→\n   148→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   149→    // State that is not checkpointed\n   150→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   151→\n   152→    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n   153→    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n   154→\n   155→    /**\n   156→     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n   157→     * state is not mergeable.\n   158→     */\n   159→    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n   160→\n   161→    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n   162→    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n   163→\n   164→    /**\n   165→     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n   166→     * timestamp.\n   167→     */\n   168→    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n   169→\n   170→    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n   171→\n   172→    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n   173→\n   174→    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n   175→\n   176→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   177→    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n   178→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   179→\n   180→    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n   181→\n   182→    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n   183→    public WindowOperator(\n   184→            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n   185→            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n   186→            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n   187→            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n   188→            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n   189→            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n   190→            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n   191→            long allowedLateness,\n   192→            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n   193→\n   194→        super(windowFunction);\n   195→\n   196→        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n   197→\n   198→        checkArgument(\n   199→                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n   200→                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n   201→\n   202→        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n   203→        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n   204→        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n   205→        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n   206→        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n   207→        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n   208→        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n   209→        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n   210→    }\n   211→\n   212→    @Override\n   213→    public void setup(\n   214→            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n   215→            StreamConfig config,\n   216→            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n   217→        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n   218→    }\n   219→\n   220→    @Override\n   221→    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n   222→        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    @Override\n   226→    public void open() throws Exception {\n   227→        super.open();\n   228→\n   229→        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n   230→        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n   231→\n   232→        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n   233→\n   234→        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n   235→        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n   236→\n   237→        windowAssignerContext =\n   238→                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n   239→                    @Override\n   240→                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n   241→                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n   242→                    }\n   243→                };\n   244→\n   245→        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n   246→        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n   247→        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n   248→            windowState =\n   249→                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n   250→                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n   251→        }\n   252→\n   253→        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n   254→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   255→\n   256→            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n   257→            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n   258→                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n   259→            } else if (windowState != null) {\n   260→                throw new IllegalStateException(\n   261→                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n   262→            }\n   263→\n   264→            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   265→            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n   266→\n   267→            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n   268→                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n   269→                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n   270→\n   271→            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n   272→                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n   273→\n   274→            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n   275→            mergingSetsState =\n   276→                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n   277→                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n   278→                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n   279→            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n   280→        }\n   281→    }\n   282→\n   283→    @Override\n   284→    public void close() throws Exception {\n   285→        super.close();\n   286→        timestampedCollector = null;\n   287→        triggerContext = null;\n   288→        processContext = null;\n   289→        windowAssignerContext = null;\n   290→    }\n   291→\n   292→    @Override\n   293→    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n   294→        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n   295→                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n   296→                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n   297→\n   298→        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n   299→        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n   300→\n   301→        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n   302→\n   303→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   304→            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   305→\n   306→            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n   307→\n   308→                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n   309→                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n   310→                // actualWindow == window\n   311→                W actualWindow =\n   312→                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n   313→                                window,\n   314→                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n   315→                                    @Override\n   316→                                    public void merge(\n   317→                                            W mergeResult,\n   318→                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n   319→                                            W stateWindowResult,\n   320→                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n   321→                                            throws Exception {\n   322→\n   323→                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   324→                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n   325→                                                        <= internalTimerService\n   326→                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n   327→                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   328→                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n   329→                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n   330→                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n   331→                                                            + internalTimerService\n   332→                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n   333→                                                            + \" window: \"\n   334→                                                            + mergeResult);\n   335→                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   336→                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n   337→                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n   338→                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n   339→                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n   340→                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   341→                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n   342→                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n   343→                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n   344→                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n   345→                                                                + \" window: \"\n   346→                                                                + mergeResult);\n   347→                                            }\n   348→                                        }\n   349→\n   350→                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n   351→                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n   352→\n   353→                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n   354→\n   355→                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n   356→                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n   357→                                            triggerContext.clear();\n   358→                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n   359→                                        }\n   360→\n   361→                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n   362→                                        // state window\n   363→                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n   364→                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n   365→                                    }\n   366→                                });\n   367→\n   368→                // drop if the window is already late\n   369→                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n   370→                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n   371→                    continue;\n   372→                }\n   373→                isSkippedElement = false;\n   374→\n   375→                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n   376→                if (stateWindow == null) {\n   377→                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n   378→                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n   379→                }\n   380→\n   381→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   382→                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n   383→\n   384→                triggerContext.key = key;\n   385→                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n   386→\n   387→                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n   388→\n   389→                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   390→                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   391→                    if (contents != null) {\n   392→                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n   393→                    }\n   394→                }\n   395→\n   396→                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   397→                    windowState.clear();\n   398→                }\n   399→                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n   400→            }\n   401→\n   402→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   403→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   404→        } else {\n   405→            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n   406→\n   407→                // drop if the window is already late\n   408→                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n   409→                    continue;\n   410→                }\n   411→                isSkippedElement = false;\n   412→\n   413→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n   414→                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n   415→\n   416→                triggerContext.key = key;\n   417→                triggerContext.window = window;\n   418→\n   419→                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n   420→\n   421→                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   422→                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   423→                    if (contents != null) {\n   424→                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n   425→                    }\n   426→                }\n   427→\n   428→                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   429→                    windowState.clear();\n   430→                }\n   431→                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n   432→            }\n   433→        }\n   434→\n   435→        // side output input event if\n   436→        // element not handled by any window\n   437→        // late arriving tag has been set\n   438→        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n   439→        // element timestamp\n   440→        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n   441→            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n   442→                sideOutput(element);\n   443→            } else {\n   444→                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n   445→            }\n   446→        }\n   447→    }\n   448→\n   449→    @Override\n   450→    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n   451→        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n   452→        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n   453→\n   454→        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n   455→\n   456→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   457→            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   458→            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n   459→            if (stateWindow == null) {\n   460→                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n   461→                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n   462→                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n   463→                return;\n   464→            } else {\n   465→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   466→            }\n   467→        } else {\n   468→            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n   469→            mergingWindows = null;\n   470→        }\n   471→\n   472→        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n   473→\n   474→        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   475→            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   476→            if (contents != null) {\n   477→                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n   478→            }\n   479→        }\n   480→\n   481→        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   482→            windowState.clear();\n   483→        }\n   484→\n   485→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   486→                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n   487→            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n   488→        }\n   489→\n   490→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   491→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   492→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   493→        }\n   494→    }\n   495→\n   496→    @Override\n   497→    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n   498→        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n   499→        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n   500→\n   501→        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n   502→\n   503→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   504→            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   505→            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n   506→            if (stateWindow == null) {\n   507→                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n   508→                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n   509→                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n   510→                return;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   513→            }\n   514→        } else {\n   515→            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n   516→            mergingWindows = null;\n   517→        }\n   518→\n   519→        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n   520→\n   521→        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   522→            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   523→            if (contents != null) {\n   524→                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n   525→            }\n   526→        }\n   527→\n   528→        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   529→            windowState.clear();\n   530→        }\n   531→\n   532→        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   533→                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n   534→            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n   535→        }\n   536→\n   537→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   538→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   539→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   540→        }\n   541→    }\n   542→\n   543→    @Override\n   544→    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n   545→        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n   546→                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n   547→                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n   548→        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n   549→                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n   550→                .build();\n   551→    }\n   552→\n   553→    /**\n   554→     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n   555→     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n   556→     *\n   557→     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n   558→     * triggerContext object.\n   559→     */\n   560→    private void clearAllState(\n   561→            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n   562→            throws Exception {\n   563→        windowState.clear();\n   564→        triggerContext.clear();\n   565→        processContext.window = window;\n   566→        processContext.clear();\n   567→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   568→            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n   569→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   570→        }\n   571→    }\n   572→\n   573→    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n   574→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   575→    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n   576→        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n   577→        processContext.window = window;\n   578→        userFunction.process(\n   579→                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n   580→    }\n   581→\n   582→    /**\n   583→     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n   584→     *\n   585→     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n   586→     */\n   587→    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n   588→        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n   589→    }\n   590→\n   591→    /**\n   592→     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n   593→     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n   594→     *\n   595→     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n   596→     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n   597→     */\n   598→    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n   599→        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   600→        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n   601→                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n   602→        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n   603→    }\n   604→\n   605→    /**\n   606→     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n   607→     * the given window.\n   608→     */\n   609→    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n   610→        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   611→                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n   612→    }\n   613→\n   614→    /**\n   615→     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n   616→     *\n   617→     * @param element The element to check\n   618→     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n   619→     */\n   620→    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n   621→        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n   622→                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n   623→                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n   624→    }\n   625→\n   626→    /**\n   627→     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n   628→     *\n   629→     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n   630→     */\n   631→    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n   632→        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n   633→        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n   634→            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n   635→            return;\n   636→        }\n   637→\n   638→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   639→            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   640→        } else {\n   641→            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   642→        }\n   643→    }\n   644→\n   645→    /**\n   646→     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n   647→     *\n   648→     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n   649→     */\n   650→    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n   651→        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n   652→        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n   653→            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n   654→            return;\n   655→        }\n   656→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   657→            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   658→        } else {\n   659→            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   660→        }\n   661→    }\n   662→\n   663→    /**\n   664→     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n   665→     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n   666→     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n   667→     *\n   668→     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n   669→     */\n   670→    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n   671→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   672→            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n   673→            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   674→        } else {\n   675→            return window.maxTimestamp();\n   676→        }\n   677→    }\n   678→\n   679→    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n   680→    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n   681→        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n   682→    }\n   683→\n   684→    /**\n   685→     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n                .build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n     * triggerContext object.\n     */\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n        processContext.window = window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n     *\n     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n     */\n    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n     */\n    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n     * the given window.\n     */\n    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n     *\n     * @param element The element to check\n     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n     */\n    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n            return;\n        }\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n     */\n    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        } else {\n            return window.maxTimestamp();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n     * state access for {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n\n        // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n        // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n        protected W window;\n\n        public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(\n                    keyedStateBackend,\n                    new SerializerFactory() {\n                        @Override\n                        public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n                                TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n                            return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n                                    executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\n                        }\n                    });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\n     */\n    public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public MergingWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\n                AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Regular per-window state store for use with {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public class PerWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public PerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor)\n                throws Exception {\n            return keyedStateBackend.getPartitionedState(window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A utility class for handling {@code ProcessWindowFunction} invocations. This can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code WindowContext}.\n     */\n    public class WindowContext implements InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext {\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected AbstractPerWindowStateStore windowState;\n\n        public WindowContext(W window) {\n            this.window = window;\n            this.windowState =\n                    windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner\n                            ? new MergingWindowStateStore(\n                                    getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig())\n                            : new PerWindowStateStore(getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"WindowContext{Window = \" + window.toString() + \"}\";\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            userFunction.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore windowState() {\n            this.windowState.window = this.window;\n            return this.windowState;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore globalState() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getKeyedStateStore();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <X> void output(OutputTag<X> outputTag, X value) {\n            if (outputTag == null) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"OutputTag must not be null.\");\n            }\n            output.collect(outputTag, new StreamRecord<>(value, window.maxTimestamp()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code Context} is a utility for handling {@code Trigger} invocations. It can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code Context}\n     */\n    public class Context implements Trigger.OnMergeContext {\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected Collection<W> mergedWindows;\n\n        public Context(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            try {\n                return WindowOperator.this.getPartitionedState(\n                        window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n            } catch (Exception e) {\n                throw new RuntimeException(\"Could not retrieve state\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <S extends MergingState<?, ?>> void mergePartitionedState(\n                StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            if (mergedWindows != null && mergedWindows.size() > 0) {\n                try {\n                    S rawState =\n                            getKeyedStateBackend()\n                                    .getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n\n                    if (rawState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n                        InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?> mergingState =\n                                (InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?>) rawState;\n                        mergingState.mergeNamespaces(window, mergedWindows);\n                    } else {\n                        throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n                                \"The given state descriptor does not refer to a mergeable state (MergingState)\");\n                    }\n                } catch (Exception e) {\n                    throw new RuntimeException(\"Error while merging state.\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onElement(element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onProcessingTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onEventTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void onMerge(Collection<W> mergedWindows) throws Exception {\n            this.mergedWindows = mergedWindows;\n            trigger.onMerge(window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            trigger.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"Context{\" + \"key=\" + key + \", window=\" + window + '}';\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Internal class for keeping track of in-flight timers. */\n    protected static class Timer<K, W extends Window> implements Comparable<Timer<K, W>> {\n        protected long timestamp;\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public Timer(long timestamp, K key, W window) {\n            this.timestamp = timestamp;\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int compareTo(Timer<K, W> o) {\n            return Long.compare(this.timestamp, o.timestamp);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean equals(Object o) {\n            if (this == o) {\n                return true;\n            }\n            if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            Timer<?, ?> timer = (Timer<?, ?>) o;\n\n            return timestamp == timer.timestamp\n                    && key.equals(timer.key)\n                    && window.equals(timer.window);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int hashCode() {\n            int result = (int) (timestamp ^ (timestamp >>> 32));\n            result = 31 * result + key.hashCode();\n            result = 31 * result + window.hashCode();\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"Timer{\"\n                    + \"timestamp=\"\n                    + timestamp\n                    + \", key=\"\n                    + key\n                    + \", window=\"\n                    + window\n                    + '}';\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Let me analyze the `processElement` method starting at line 293:\n\nFor merging windows (lines 303-403):\n1. The code iterates through elementWindows (lines 306-400)\n2. For each window, it calls `mergingWindows.addWindow()` which can merge the window with existing windows\n3. Then at line 369, it checks `if (isWindowLate(actualWindow))` to see if the window is already late\n4. If the window is late, it retires the window and continues (skips processing)\n5. Only if the window is NOT late does it add the element to the window state and process it\n\nThen AFTER the merging window loop (lines 440-446):\n```java\n// side output input event if\n// element not handled by any window\n// late arriving tag has been set\n// windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n// element timestamp\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe problem is:\n1. For merging windows, an element arrives that could extend a session window (merge into an existing window)\n2. The element has a timestamp past the current watermark\n3. In the merging window loop, when it checks `if (isWindowLate(actualWindow))` at line 369, this returns true because the merged window's max timestamp + allowedLateness <= current watermark\n4. The element is skipped (isSkippedElement remains true) for that window\n5. Later, the code checks `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` at line 440\n6. If both are true AND lateDataOutputTag is set, it calls `sideOutput(element)`\n\n\n7. However, the `sideOutput` method at line 587 calls `output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element)`, but the output collector may not have this tag properly registered, causing the element to be silently dropped instead of being routed to the side output.\n\nThe core issue appears to be that for merging windows, a late-arriving element could potentially extend an existing window that hasn't been cleaned up yet. When the code attempts to emit this element via `sideOutput()`, the `lateDataOutputTag` may be null or unregistered at that point in the execution path, so the element gets lost rather than properly handled.\n\nThe fix should ensure that before marking an element as late, we first check whether it could be merged into a still-active window. Currently the logic checks if the resulting merged window is late after the merge happens, which causes valid elements to be rejected. The order needs to flip—verify the element can extend an existing window before applying the late-element logic. Let me trace through the actual test execution to understand what's happening. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01GTdY6tVSJwreHFieXCQ5N1","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now let me try to understand the bug better by looking at the code more carefully. Let me re-read the processElement method and analyze the flow for merging windows.\n\nLooking at lines 303-403 in WindowOperator.java:\n\n```java\nif (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n    MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n        // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n        // actualWindow == window\n        W actualWindow =\n                mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                        window,\n                        new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                            // ... merge logic ...\n                        });\n\n        // drop if the window is already late\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n            continue;\n        }\n        isSkippedElement = false;\n\n        W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n        if (stateWindow == null) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\n                    \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n        }\n\n        windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n        windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n        triggerContext.key = key;\n        triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n        registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n    }\n\n    // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n    mergingWindows.persist();\n}\n```\n\nThen at the end (lines 440-446), there's the late element handling:\n\n```java\n// side output input event if\n// element not handled by any window\n// late arriving tag has been set\n// windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n// element timestamp\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element\n```\n\nI see the issue now. When a merging window assigner processes an element, `isSkippedElement` gets set to `false` inside the loop whenever a window is added. But if all windows end up being late and get retired via `mergingWindows.retireWindow()`, the `isSkippedElement` flag never gets reset back to `true`. This means late elements that should be side-output are instead silently dropped because the condition `isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)` evaluates to false. Looking at the `isWindowLate` method, it determines if a window has passed its cleanup deadline by comparing the window's max timestamp plus allowed lateness against the current watermark. The issue becomes clearer when considering merging windows: an element arriving after the watermark could still extend an existing window that hasn't expired, but the code treats the merged window as late and skips it entirely, preventing the element from being processed or properly side-outputted if the late data tag isn't available. accept it. If the merged window's end timestamp plus allowed lateness is at or before the current watermark, we reject it as late.\n\nThe real issue becomes clearer: when an element arrives that could extend an existing session window, we're checking lateness on the merged result. If that merged window is considered late, the element gets rejected to a side output even though it legitimately extends an active window. The merge operation happens before the lateness check, but the problem is we're applying the lateness logic to a window that's being extended by new data. be side-outputted since the merged window exceeds the allowed lateness threshold. The key insight is that once the watermark passes the window's max timestamp plus allowed lateness, any subsequent elements attempting to merge into that window will be rejected. This explains why Element 2 gets dropped even though it arrives within the session gap—the window has already been marked as late by the advancing watermark. an element is considered late when `element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= watermark`.\n\nLooking at the bug description more carefully, the problem seems to be that when a late element arrives, it could potentially be merged into an existing window that hasn't been cleaned up yet. The code rejects it as late before attempting the merge, then tries to side output it, but the lateDataOutputTag might be null or the output collector doesn't have the tag registered, causing the element to be silently dropped instead of being properly handled.\n\nExamining the conditional logic at lines 440-446, the side output only executes when three conditions are met: the element wasn't handled by any window, the element is actually late, and the lateDataOutputTag is not null. But what if the scenario is different—what if the element itself isn't late, yet the window it would merge into is considered late? In that case, the element gets rejected from merging, but the late element handling code path never triggers because the element's timestamp hasn't crossed the lateness threshold.\n\nConsider: a session window [100, 110] exists, watermark reaches 115 (making the window late), and an element with timestamp 108 arrives. The element would merge the window to [100, 115], but since that merged window is now late, it's rejected. The element is marked as skipped, and since 108 + 0 <= 115, it should be routed to the side output—but only if lateDataOutputTag is configured. The issue might be that lateDataOutputTag is null, or perhaps the condition for detecting element lateness isn't being evaluated correctly in the merging windows context.\n\nLooking more carefully at the code structure: non-merging windows use `continue` when a window is late, which preserves isSkippedElement as true and triggers the side output. But in the merging windows path, the late window check happens differently—the element might be getting rejected without properly setting isSkippedElement to false, or the side output logic isn't being reached at all in that branch.\n\nThe real issue seems to be about the order of operations for merging windows. The task description hints that we need to check whether an element can merge into an existing non-expired window *before* deciding it's late. Currently, the code appears to be merging first and then checking if the resulting window is late, but it should validate that the target window itself isn't already expired before accepting the merge.\n\nIn a session window scenario, if window [100, 110] exists and watermark reaches 111, the window becomes late. When element at timestamp 108 arrives, it would merge to create [100, 118]. The merged window isn't late (118 > 111), so it gets accepted. But the real question is whether we should even be merging into a window that was already expired before the merge happened. The fix likely involves checking the existing window's validity before performing the merge operation.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":366,"cache_read_input_tokens":44533,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":366},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"39b9f80f-c82d-41a3-9619-098ef4fa5c2e","uuid":"e8151163-81e7-4b13-bf99-d9f9d4da668b"}

It might happen that this triggers\n   135→     * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\n   136→     * called.\n   137→     *\n   138→     * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\n   139→     * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\n   140→     * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\n   141→     * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\n   142→     * swallowed up by another window.\n   143→     *\n   144→     * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\n   145→     * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\n   146→     *\n   147→     * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\n   148→     * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\n   149→     * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. See the NOTICE file\n * distributed with this work for additional information\n * regarding copyright ownership.
