You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\n\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\n/**\n * Utility for keeping track of merging {@link Window Windows} when using a {@link\n * MergingWindowAssigner} in a {@link WindowOperator}.\n *\n * <p>When merging windows, we keep one of the original windows as the state window, i.e. the window\n * that is used as namespace to store the window elements. Elements from the state windows of merged\n * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\n * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\n *\n * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\n * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\n * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\n * of windows and state.\n *\n * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\n *\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\n */\npublic class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\n\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\n\n    /**\n     * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\n     * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\n     * prevent costly state juggling.\n     */\n    private final Map<W, W> mapping;\n\n    /**\n     * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. We use this to decide whether we need\n     * to persist any changes to state.\n     */\n    private final Map<W, W> initialMapping;\n\n    private final ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state;\n\n    /** Our window assigner. */\n    private final MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    /** Restores a {@link MergingWindowSet} from the given state. */\n    public MergingWindowSet(\n            MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner, ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state)\n            throws Exception {\n        this.windowAssigner = windowAssigner;\n        mapping = new HashMap<>();\n\n        Iterable<Tuple2<W, W>> windowState = state.get();\n        if (windowState != null) {\n            for (Tuple2<W, W> window : windowState) {\n                mapping.put(window.f0, window.f1);\n            }\n        }\n\n        this.state = state;\n\n        initialMapping = new HashMap<>();\n        initialMapping.putAll(mapping);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Persist the updated mapping to the given state if the mapping changed since initialization.\n     */\n    public void persist() throws Exception {\n        if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\n            state.update(\n                    mapping.entrySet().stream()\n                            .map((w) -> new Tuple2<>(w.getKey(), w.getValue()))\n                            .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the state window for the given in-flight {@code Window}. The state window is the\n     * {@code Window} in which we keep the actual state of a given in-flight window. Windows might\n     * expand but we keep to original state window for keeping the elements of the window to avoid\n     * costly state juggling.\n     *\n     * @param window The window for which to get the state window.\n     */\n    public W getStateWindow(W window) {\n        return mapping.get(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Removes the given window from the set of in-flight windows.\n     *\n     * @param window The {@code Window} to remove.\n     */\n    public void retireWindow(W window) {\n        W removed = this.mapping.remove(window);\n        if (removed == null) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\n                    \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Adds a new {@code Window} to the set of in-flight windows. It might happen that this triggers\n     * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\n     * called.\n     *\n     * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\n     * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\n     * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\n     * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\n     * swallowed up by another window.\n     *\n     * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\n     * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\n     *\n     * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\n     * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\n     * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. This can also be the new\n     *     {@code Window} itself in case no merge occurred.","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":255}}}

Now I understand. A session window is created with `TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout)`. So:- Element at timestamp 100 with sessionTimeout 3 creates window [100, 103)\n- Element at timestamp 102 with sessionTimeout 3 creates window [102, 105)\n- These windows merge because they overlap, creating window [100, 105)\n\nNow let me think about the bug scenario again:\n\nScenario:\n1. Element arrives at timestamp 100, creates window [100, 103) (sessionTimeout=3)\n2. Element arrives at timestamp 102, creates window [102, 105), merges into [100, 105)\n3. Watermark advances to 105\n4. Window [100, 105) is now late because maxTimestamp=105, allowedLateness=0, so 105 <= 105 is true\n5. Element arrives at timestamp 104 (which is < watermark 105, so it's late)\n   - isElementLate(104): 104 + 0 <= 105? YES, it's late\n   - Element creates window [104, 107) (sessionTimeout=3)\n   - This should merge with [100, 105) to create [100, 107)\n   - But [100, 107) is NOT late (107 > 105)\n   - However, the current code might reject it because the original window [100, 105) is late\n\nLet me recheck the code. In processElement at line 369:\n```java\nif (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\nSo we're checking isWindowLate(actualWindow), where actualWindow is the RESULT of the merge, not the original window. So in the scenario above:\n- Element [104, 107) is added\n- It merges with [100, 105) to create [100, 107)\n\n\n- We check isWindowLate([100, 107)): 107 + 0 <= 105? NO, so the window is NOT late\n- Element is accepted\n\nSo the code should already handle this correctly! But the bug description says it's not working. The issue might be with when the watermark check happens. Let me look at the merge function in WindowOperator at line 314 to see how windows are actually combined.\n\nDuring the merge operation, there's a watermark validation that checks if the merged window's end time plus allowed lateness has already passed the current watermark. If it has, an exception gets thrown. This check happens at merge time, which could be the source of the problem if the watermark advances between when the element arrives and when the merge completes. throws Exception {\n\n                                if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                        && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark())) {\n                                    throw new UnsupportedOperationException(...)\n                                }\n                                // ... rest of merge logic ...\n                            }\n                        });\n\n// drop if the window is already late\nif (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\nThe merge callback validates that the resulting window isn't late and throws an exception if it is. After the merge completes, there's another check using `isWindowLate()` that drops the window if needed. I need to trace whether the exception gets caught somewhere or if the second check is what actually handles late windows.\n\nLooking at the exception message more carefully—\"The end timestamp of an event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark\"—this is validating that `mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark` doesn't hold. The critical detail is that this validation only runs when a merge actually occurs. If a new window gets added without merging with existing windows, this check never executes.\n\nThis distinction matters: the exception path only triggers during merges, but windows can be added without merging. I should write a test case to see if this is where the inconsistency lies.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1591,"cache_read_input_tokens":58848,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1591},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"39b9f80f-c82d-41a3-9619-098ef4fa5c2e","uuid":"029268a4-64c2-43b7-a21f-e28df722d286"}

tests whether merging works correctly with the CountTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithCountTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                   new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4000));\n elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n     stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testLateness(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 500;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String,                  new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(EventTimeTrigger.create()),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                      STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1300));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness >\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1997));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this is 1 and not 3 because the trigger fires and purges\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this will be side output because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                lateExpected,\n                (Iterable) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is side output as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // this is also side output as late (we test that they are not accidentally merged)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n      // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n        ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();","numLines":160,"startLine":293,"totalLines":1017}}}

                          stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testLateness(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 500;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String,                          stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(EventTimeTrigger.create()),\n                      STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1300));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness >\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1997));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this is 1 and not 3 because the trigger fires and purges\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this will be side output because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                lateExpected,\n                (Iterable) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n 10100));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new      // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>)            for (W mergedWindow : mergedWindows) {\n                W res = this.mapping.remove(mergedWindow);\n                if (res != null) {\n                    mergedStateWindows.add(res);\n                }\n            }\n\n            this.mapping.put(mergeResult, mergedStateWindow);\n\n            // don't put the target state window into the merged windows\n            mergedStateWindows.remove(mergedStateWindow);\n\n            // don't merge the new window itself, it never had any state associated with it\n            // i.e. if we are only merging one pre-existing window into itself\n            // without extending the pre-existing window\n            if (!(mergedWindows.contains(mergeResult) && mergedWindows.size() == 1)) {\n                mergeFunction.merge(\n                        mergeResult,\n                        mergedWindows,\n                        this.mapping.get(mergeResult),\n                        mergedStateWindows);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // the new window created a new, self-contained window without merging\n        if (mergeResults.isEmpty() || (resultWindow.equals(newWindow) && !mergedNewWindow)) {\n            this.mapping.put(resultWindow, resultWindow);\n        }\n\n        return resultWindow;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Callback for {@link #addWindow(Window, MergeFunction)}.\n     *\n     * @param <W>\n     */\n    public interface MergeFunction<W> {\n","numLines":80,"startLine":153,"totalLines":255}}}

    new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late-output\") {};\n\n    private void testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n      Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new     stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n                   operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testContinuousWatermarkTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n     stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                       STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String,                  new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                               String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(EventTimeTrigger.create()),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                      STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1300));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness >\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1997));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this is 1 and not 3 because the trigger fires and purges\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this will be side output because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                lateExpected,\n                (Iterable) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name element due to lateness\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3001));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3001));\n\n
