tests whether merging works correctly with the CountTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithCountTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the ContinuousEventTimeTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithContinuousEventTimeTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4000));\n elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n            testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 2500));\n\n testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            expectedOutput.add(new                   operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private {\n        try {\n            return AsyncKeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness.create(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testContinuousWatermarkTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        GlobalWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                new GlobalWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, GlobalWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, GlobalWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n   ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // The global window actually ignores these timestamps...\n\n TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(8000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is side output as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new are not accidentally merged)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testNotSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() throws Exception {\n        // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n               new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onMerge(TimeWindow window, OnMergeContext ctx) {\n            ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // For merging windows, check if the element is late before adding to the window set\n                // If late, skip processing to allow side output of the element\n                if (isElementLate(element)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the\n                // actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n    @Override\n    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n                .build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n     * triggerContext object.\n     */\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n            return;\n        }\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n     */\n    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        } else {\n            return window.maxTimestamp();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}.
